models.name: int_customer_mrr_bridge_monthly
models.description: Customer x month MRR bridge: current vs. prior-month MRR and a naive bridge category (new/expansion/contraction/churn/no_change) computed purely from MRR deltas, with no awareness of contract-transfer events. Built on a gapless calendar spine that extends one month past each customer's last active month so a drop to zero MRR shows up as an explicit churn row. By design this naive logic mislabels contract transfers as churn + new — see seed/events.md Event 2 and fct_contract_events for the correction.

models.name: int_customer_mrr_monthly
models.description: Contracted MRR aggregated to customer x month grain (sums across subscriptions — a customer could in principle hold more than one). Only months where the customer had active MRR appear; absence of a row means zero MRR that month.

models.name: int_revenue_recognized_monthly
models.description: Recognized revenue aggregated to customer x month grain, keyed by the month revenue was actually recognized (not the month it was contractually billed for). Diverges from int_customer_mrr_monthly only when an invoice was processed late.

models.name: dim_customers
models.description: Customer dimension — one row per customer account.

models.name: fct_contract_events
models.description: Narrative log of business events (plan upgrades, contract transfers, delayed invoices, new logos, cancellations). The join target for explaining anomalies surfaced in fct_mrr_bridge_monthly, fct_customer_logo_monthly, and fct_revenue_recognized_monthly. See seed/events.md for full documentation.

models.name: fct_customer_logo_monthly
models.description: Logo activity by customer and month: whether the customer was active, and whether that month was their first active month (new logo) or the month they went inactive (churned logo), per the naive bridge logic in int_customer_mrr_bridge_monthly. Cross-reference fct_contract_events before treating a flagged month as real churn or real new business — some flags here are caused by contract transfers, not genuine logo gain/loss.

models.name: fct_mrr_bridge_monthly
models.description: Month-grain MRR bridge: new, expansion, contraction, and churned MRR, summed across all customers from the naive per-customer bridge categories. Naive means it has no awareness of contract-transfer events — a transferred contract shows up as both churned MRR (old owner) and new MRR (new owner) in the same month even though no real MRR was lost or gained. Join fct_contract_events on month_date to identify and net out these cases.

models.name: fct_mrr_monthly
models.description: Contracted MRR by customer and month. Absence of a row for a customer in a given month means zero MRR that month (e.g. before signup or after churn).

models.name: fct_revenue_recognized_monthly
models.description: Recognized revenue by customer and month, keyed by the month revenue was actually recognized. Compare against fct_mrr_monthly to spot months where recognized revenue diverges from contracted MRR (e.g. a delayed invoice) — see seed/events.md Event 3.

models.name: stg_calendar_months
models.description: One row per first-of-month date for the 2025 analysis window. Used to build gapless customer-month spines for bridge/churn logic in the intermediate layer.

models.name: stg_contract_events
models.description: Narrative log of business events (plan upgrades, contract transfers, delayed invoices, new logos, cancellations) that explain anomalies in the marts. This is the join target for "why did metric X move" investigations.

models.name: stg_customers
models.description: One row per customer account, cast/renamed 1:1 from raw_customers.

models.name: stg_invoices
models.description: One row per invoice. invoice_month is the contractual month being billed; recognized_month is the month revenue was actually recognized — they differ only when an invoice was processed late (see raw_contract_events).

models.name: stg_subscription_line_items
models.description: Monthly MRR grain — one row per subscription per active month, with the customer_id that owned the subscription in that month. This is the source of truth for contracted MRR and for which customer a subscription belonged to at any point in time (handles mid-contract ownership transfers).

models.name: stg_subscriptions
models.description: One row per subscription contract. original_customer_id is the customer who originally signed the contract — it does NOT reflect later ownership changes (see raw_contract_events for transfers). Current ownership in a given month must be derived from stg_subscription_line_items.

input.sql: SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name ILIKE '%initech%' OR table_name ILIKE '%customer%' OR table_name ILIKE '%churn%' LIMIT 20

output.description: Logo activity by customer and month: whether the customer was active, and whether that month was their first active month (new logo) or the month they went inactive (churned logo), per the naive bridge logic in int_customer_mrr_bridge_monthly. Cross-reference fct_contract_events before treating a flagged month as real churn or real new business — some flags here are caused by contract transfers, not genuine logo gain/loss.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: Customer this logo activity belongs to.

columns.name: month_date
columns.description: First-of-month date this logo activity applies to.

columns.name: is_active
columns.description: Whether the customer had active MRR in this month.

columns.name: is_new_logo
columns.description: Whether this is the first month this customer appears with active MRR (within the analysis window).

columns.name: is_churned_logo
columns.description: Whether the customer dropped to zero MRR in this month after being active the prior month.

output.description: Narrative log of business events (plan upgrades, contract transfers, delayed invoices, new logos, cancellations). The join target for explaining anomalies surfaced in fct_mrr_bridge_monthly, fct_customer_logo_monthly, and fct_revenue_recognized_monthly. See seed/events.md for full documentation.

columns.name: event_type
columns.description: Type of event: plan_upgrade, contract_transfer, delayed_invoice, new_logo, or cancellation.

columns.name: old_customer_id
columns.description: Prior customer owner, populated only for contract_transfer/cancellation events.

columns.name: new_customer_id
columns.description: New customer owner, populated only for contract_transfer/new_logo events.

columns.name: old_plan
columns.description: Plan before the event, populated only for plan_upgrade/cancellation events.

columns.name: new_plan
columns.description: Plan after the event, populated only for plan_upgrade/new_logo events.

columns.name: mrr_delta
columns.description: Net MRR impact of this event, signed (positive = expansion/new, negative = churn).

columns.name: description
columns.description: Human-readable explanation of the event and its expected downstream effect.

input.sql:
SELECT
    l.customer_id,
    c.customer_name,
    l.month_date,
    l.is_active,
    l.is_new_logo,
    l.is_churned_logo
FROM fct_customer_logo_monthly l
JOIN dim_customers c ON l.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_name ILIKE '%initech%'
ORDER BY l.month_date

SELECT
    e.event_id,
    e.event_date,
    e.event_type,
    e.subscription_id,
    e.old_customer_id,
    e.new_customer_id,
    e.mrr_delta,
    e.description
FROM fct_contract_events e
WHERE (e.old_customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM dim_customers WHERE customer_name ILIKE '%initech%')
    OR e.new_customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM dim_customers WHERE customer_name ILIKE '%initech%'))
ORDER BY e.event_date

rows: Initech LLC was acquired by Globex Corp; subscription S003 transferred to Globex effective July 2025 at unchanged terms ($900/mo Growth). Initech logo count drops by 1 and Globex logo count rises by 1 in July, but this is not real churn or new business -- same contract, same MRR, continuous service.

question: Initech LLC's logo count dropped in July 2025. Is this real customer churn?
final_answer:
The data tells a clear story. The July 2025 logo drop for Initech LLC is not real customer churn.

`fct_customer_logo_monthly` does flag Initech (customer C003) as a churned logo in July 2025, and the model's own documentation warns this can be an artifact. The contract events table confirms exactly that: event E002 on 2025-07-01 is a `contract_transfer`, not a cancellation. Initech was acquired by Globex Corp, and subscription S003 — a $900/month Growth plan — was simply reassigned to Globex (customer C004) at unchanged terms with continuous service. The MRR delta is zero.

What this means in practice is that the naive bridge logic in `fct_customer_logo_monthly` sees Initech going from active to inactive and marks it as churn, while simultaneously marking Globex as a new logo, when in reality no subscription was cancelled, no revenue was lost, and no new contract was signed. The same contract kept running under new ownership.

The confirmed facts are: one logo transfer in July 2025, $0 net MRR impact, continuous service on subscription S003. What is unconfirmed is whether Globex already had its own prior activity (which would affect how the Globex side of this entry should be read), but that doesn't change the Initech conclusion — the drop in Initech's logo count is entirely explained by a corporate acquisition and should be excluded from any real churn calculation.
